The surrounding reef walls, drift corridors, and sandy flats add sharks, turtles, and macro life to one of diving's most concentrated wildlife experiences.

name: Salvador da Bahia
description: Salvador da Bahia sits on the northeastern Brazilian coast where warm tropical waters meet the South Atlantic. Reef fish diversity is exceptional year-round, and between July and November whale sharks patrol the outer reefs near the Todos os Santos Bay mouth. Seahorses, frogfish, and nudibranchs reward patient macro divers among the rubble and sea grass beds.

name: Ilha Grande
description: Ilha Grande lies off the Rio de Janeiro coastline inside a state park and APA marine reserve, keeping its Atlantic reef largely pristine. Green and hawksbill turtles are resident, endemic Brazilian reef fish such as the Brazilian grunt and rock beauty are abundant, and the wreck of the Iate Clube freighter adds a wreck-diving dimension within recreational limits.

name: Príncipe Island
description: One of the most remote and pristine dive destinations in the Atlantic, Príncipe is a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve rising from the Gulf of Guinea. Its volcanic seamounts and undisturbed reefs host endemic species found nowhere else on Earth, alongside seasonal whale sharks and large pelagics. With very few divers visiting each year, the underwater landscapes here feel genuinely untouched — dense sea fans, healthy hard coral, and extraordinary fish biomass on every dive.

name: Kosterfjord
description: Kosterfjord is home to Sweden's first and only marine national park, Kosterhavet, established in 2009. The fjord plunges to over 240 metres and channels nutrient-rich Atlantic water from the Norwegian Trench — making it the most species-rich marine environment in Scandinavia. Cold, clear water feeds extraordinary macro life: hundreds of nudibranch species, sea spiders, horse mussels the size of dinner plates, and dense fields of sea urchins carpeting rock walls. Dead man's fingers soft coral and sea fans colonise near-vertical walls. The protected status means visibility and biodiversity are exceptional for a temperate site.

name: Lysefjord / Stavanger
description: The Stavanger region offers some of Northern Europe's most dramatic cold-water diving. Lysefjord cuts 42 kilometres inland beneath vertical cliffs rising 600 metres, and its subsurface walls are equally steep — dropping into darkness colonised by dead man's fingers, sea anemones, and kelp forests. Wolf fish patrol rocky overhangs, Atlantic cod school in the shallows, and nudibranchs of every colour cling to encrusted walls. Several accessible wrecks near Stavanger harbour add a wreck-diving dimension. Summer brings extended daylight, lifting water temperatures enough for productive dry-suit dives with long surface intervals in jaw-dropping scenery.

name: Camiguin
description: Camiguin is a small volcanic island off the northern coast of Mindanao, and its diving is shaped entirely by that geology. The Sunken Cemetery — a cross-marked reef where an 1871 eruption swallowed a coastal graveyard — is one of the Philippines' most atmospheric dives. Whale sharks visit the waters near Balingoan channel with regularity between November and May, and the island's lack of mass tourism means reefs still carry dense hard coral and schooling fish that have largely vanished elsewhere in the Visayas. Black sand slopes host exceptional muck fauna, and the nearby Mantigue Island marine sanctuary gives every dive a ribbon of colour that is hard to match in Mindanao.

name: Pescador Island
description: A limestone pinnacle rising off the southwest coast of Cebu, Pescador Island packs an extraordinary range of marine life into a compact area. The sardine run — locally called the Sardine Ball or Bait Ball — draws thresher sharks, jacks, and barracuda into a choreographed feeding frenzy that can materialise at any depth from 5 to 30 metres. The island's walls drop to beyond 40 metres and are draped in sea fans and black coral, sheltering pygmy seahorses, frogfish, and ghost pipefish in their branches. Moray eels of several species occupy nearly every crack in the reef. Pescador is reachable in 20 minutes by banca from Moalboal, and the consistent current keeps the water clear and the plankton thick enough to support this concentration of life year-round.

name: Tomini Bay, Togian Islands
description: One of Indonesia's least visited dive destinations, Togian Islands sits inside the Gulf of Tomini where three ocean currents converge. Pristine reefs with almost no diver pressure, extraordinary macro life, and sunken WWII wrecks rarely seen by outsiders.

name: Rinca Island
description: Sister island to Komodo in the Lesser Sunda chain, Rinca is home to Komodo dragons on land and some of the Komodo National Park's best manta ray cleaning stations, schooling fish aggregations, and fast drift dives beneath the surface.
